lede: A look at December 30 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Igor Shesterkin and Kristin Kreuk, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Lanzhou–Zhengzhou–Changsha pipeline
events.desc: A segment of the Lanzhou–Zhengzhou–Changsha pipeline ruptures in Shaanxi, China, and approximately 150,000 L (40,000 US gal) of diesel oil flows down the Wei River before finally reaching the Yellow River.

events.title: MV Senopati Nusantara
events.desc: The Indonesian passenger ferry MV Senopati Nusantara sinks in a storm, resulting in at least 400 deaths.

events.title: Tropical Storm Zeta
events.desc: Tropical Storm Zeta forms in the open Atlantic Ocean, tying the record for the latest tropical cyclone ever to form in the North Atlantic basin.

events.title: Rizal Day bombings
events.desc: Rizal Day bombings: A series of bombs explode in various places in Metro Manila, Philippines within a period of a few hours, killing 22 and injuring about a hundred.

events.title: Guatemalan Air Force
events.desc: The Guatemalan Air Force sinks several Mexican fishing boats alleged to have breached maritime borders, killing three and sparking international tension.

events.title: National Bureau of Investigation (Finland)
events.desc: The Finnish National Bureau of Investigation is established to consolidate criminal investigation and intelligence into a single agency.

events.title: All-India Muslim League
events.desc: The All-India Muslim League is founded in Dacca, East Bengal, British India (later Dhaka, Bangladesh).

events.title: Iroquois Theatre fire
events.desc: A fire at the Iroquois Theater in Chicago, Illinois kills at least 605.

events.title: Discovery Expedition
events.desc: The Discovery Expedition under Robert Falcon Scott attains a Farthest South at 82°17′S in Antarctica.

events.title: War of 1812
events.desc: War of 1812: British soldiers burn Buffalo, New York.

births.name: S. Jithesh
births.line: Indian Speed Cartoonist and Pictorial Orator

deaths.text: Khaleda Zia, Bangladeshi Politician, Former Prime Minister of Bangladesh
Beate Sirota Gordon, Austrian-American director and producer
Saddam Hussein, Iraqi general and politician, fifth President of Iraq
Trygve Lie, Norwegian journalist and politician, first Secretary-General of the United Nations
Nicholas, Count of Taaffe, 6th Viscount Taaffe, Irish-Austrian soldier and courtier
